objective.description: Cost is optimized only within per-task-type quality guardrails. A cheap wrong answer is more expensive than a costly correct one.

providers._doc: How models resolve to credentials. Calls go through a self-hosted LiteLLM proxy (routing/fallback OFF — the Gates decide). LiteLLM holds direct provider keys; any model without a direct key falls through to OpenRouter. Full setup in SECRETS_SETUP.md, hierarchy in API_KEY_HIERARCHY.md. Keys are referenced by env-var NAME only — never store raw key values in this repo.
providers.resolution_rule: For any model: look up its provider -> look up that provider's key_env -> read the key from the environment / Secrets Manager at runtime. If the provider key is unset OR the direct call fails/rate-limits, LiteLLM routes the same request via OPENROUTER_API_KEY.

tier_0_architect.purpose: Builds and periodically re-tunes the orchestration system from benchmark data. NOT in the request hot path.
tier_0_architect.runs: on-demand or scheduled (e.g. nightly review of benchmark logs)
responsibilities: Read benchmark logs (predicted vs actual complexity, cost, quality)
Detect routing rules that consistently under/over-shoot
Update rules-and-gates and model-tier-matrix via PR

tier_1_planner.purpose: Front line. Reads the task, runs clarifying Q&A with the human until context is sufficient, scores complexity, emits a routing plan.
responsibilities: Receive callbacks from micro-orchestrators and re-plan

tier_2_executors.purpose: Do the actual work. Selected by complexity score.

tier_2_5_micro_orchestrator.purpose: Sits beside each executor. On failure, makes ONE fast local decision: reroute to a sibling fixer, or escalate back to the planner. Hard-capped communications to prevent loops.
tier_2_5_micro_orchestrator.activates_on: executor failure, confidence drop, or lint/type failure

decision.local_fix: route error to one cheaper fixer for a single shot
decision.callback: report 'complexity underestimated, actual N/10' to planner for re-plan

tier_3_verifier.purpose: Validates executor output before it is committed downstream.
checks: second-opinion: independent model review for high-stakes tasks (Gate H)

tier_failover.purpose: Catch-all when a primary provider is rate-limited or down. Accepts higher cost only because primaries are unavailable.
tier_failover.trigger: primary + secondary for a tier both unavailable
tier_failover.note: Never the default. Cost penalty is the reason it is last.

timeout.strategy: retry once with shorter context, then advance to next in chain

rate_limit.strategy: skip immediately to next provider in chain
rate_limit.do_not: back off and retry same provider in hot path

invalid_response.strategy: one reformulation attempt, then escalate one tier

crash_or_5xx.strategy: advance to next provider in chain, log incident
crash_or_5xx.note: provider crashes (e.g. GLM/DeepSeek peak-hour outages) are why chains bounce between independent providers

compression.description: Optional, per-layer context compression. Lossy by nature at some layers, so the toggle is stakes-aware, not a dumb on/off. Full rationale and rules in compression-rules.md.
compression._stakes_override_note: When a task is high-stakes (Gate H), force compression OFF at executor and verifier regardless of layer toggles below.

planner.note: squash clarifying Q&A into a tight spec before planning

executor.note: dedup + snippet/summary + diff-only; never drop substance

micro_orch.note: distill a failure to 'what broke + why'

verifier.note: needs full fidelity to check correctness; default OFF

handoff.note: inter-agent payloads: IDs + summaries, no whole files

modes.lossless: safe transforms only: dedup, strip boilerplate, reference-by-ID, diff instead of whole file. Reversible-ish, no meaning dropped.
modes.lossy: summarization / distillation by a small model. Drops detail. Opt-in per layer, never the global default.

compression.cost_note: Lossy compression usually costs an extra small-model call. Net win only when downstream token savings beat that call cost, hence min_tokens_to_compress. Benchmarked like any other rule; the Architect auto-disables compression at any layer where it drops quality_pass_rate for a task type.
